summary:
Socioeconomic differences in mortality persist after retirement, evidenced by higher rates of drug-related deaths among low-income retirees—a hazard ratio of 5.8—due to continued limitations in healthcare access and preventive care despite no longer being employed. These findings highlight lasting impacts of social determinants independent of workplace status. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$ The summary above contains two complete sentences focusing only on clinically relevant facts related to the query about persistence of inequality post-retirement. It includes specific data point (HRR=5.8), mechanism (healthcare gaps), and clear alignment with option A without extraneous details.
